attributes.description: Alerts when Lambda fails to write events to the configured dead-letter queue, meaning failed events may be lost.
attributes.name: [AWS Lambda OTel] Dead letter errors

investigation_guide.blob:
## Dead Letter Errors

### Triage Steps
1. Verify the DLQ (SQS or SNS) exists and the Lambda execution role has publish permissions.
2. Check DLQ resource policies and encryption (KMS) configuration.
3. Review whether the DLQ itself is full or throttled.
4. Inspect recent function errors that should have been routed to the DLQ.
5. Confirm DLQ ARN in the function configuration matches the intended target.

### Escalation
DLQ write failures mean the safety net is broken — failed events are being dropped.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.lambda.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/Lambda"
    AND MetricName == "DeadLetterErrors"
    AND stat == "Sum"
| STATS total_dlq_errors = SUM(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/Lambda/DeadLetterErrors`)
  BY FunctionName, cloud.region
// Any DLQ write failure is critical — failed events may be lost
| WHERE total_dlq_errors > 0
| EVAL function_id = CONCAT(FunctionName, "/", cloud.region)
| SORT total_dlq_errors DESC
| LIMIT 10
